title: Filling Jobs
content:
Overview


When a student needs a tutor for a subject but hasn't found one through the Scheduling section, support agents help match them with an available tutor. This process is called "Filling a Job." Agents act as the bridge between the student's request and a willing tutor.



When to Use This Process


  • A student contacts support requesting a tutor for a specific subject.
  • The student has already purchased credits or a package and is ready to book.
  • The student was unable to find a suitable tutor through self-service scheduling.
  • A previous tutor match fell through and the student needs a replacement.


Agent Procedure — Filling a Job


  1. Gather student information: Confirm the student's full name, email, subject needed, grade level, and preferred availability (days and times).
  2. Verify credits: Check the student's account in User Lookup to confirm they have available credits or an active package. If they don't, direct them to purchase credits first — refer to the Subscriptions & Packages article.
  3. Search for tutors: Review available tutors who teach the requested subject. Check their availability, qualifications, and grade level compatibility.
  4. Contact tutors: Reach out to one or more qualified tutors. This may be done via email, chat, or phone depending on your team's workflow. Confirm their willingness to take on the student.
  5. Confirm the match: Once a tutor agrees, document the match. Inform the student of the tutor's name and the subject they teach.
  6. Instruct the student: Direct the student to the Scheduling section of their dashboard to find the tutor and book a session. Remind them that credits are required before booking.
  7. Create a ticket: Log the job in the Ticketing section under the Account category with the subject line including the student's email and subject (e.g., "Job Filled — student@email.com — Algebra 1"). Document which tutor was matched.


Tutor No-Show or Fallthrough


If a matched tutor fails to confirm, becomes unavailable, or the match falls through for any reason:


  • Return to the tutor list and contact the next qualified tutor.
  • Update the ticket with the new tutor information.
  • Inform the student of the change.

If a tutor confirms a session but fails to attend, refer to the Booking a Session article for the no-show policy — the student's credit is refunded and the tutor must provide a free make-up session.



Key Talking Points for Agents


  • "Let me check which tutors are available for [subject]."
  • "I'll reach out to a few tutors and get back to you with options."
  • "Once I confirm a tutor, you'll find them in the Scheduling section of your dashboard."
  • "Make sure you have credits available so you can book right away."
  • "I'll send you an update within 24 hours."
